content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 6, 2016

Simi Valley Public Cemetery
Attention: Leigh-anne Harrison-Bigbie
1461 Thompson Lane,
Simi Valley, CA 93065

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-16-083

Dear Ms. Harrison-Bigbie:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090.

QUESTION

As a Cemetery Manager do the Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit you from selling pre-need insurance?

CONCLUSION

No, the Act does not prohibit you from selling pre-need insurance to Cemetery customers.

FACTS

You are the Cemetery Manager at Simi Valley Public Cemetery in Simi Valley. As the Cemetery Manger you receive a salary of $1,340.00 as well as other compensation of living in a house on site with all utilities paid, to perform your part time duties of operating the cemetery. Your duties as Cemetery Manager primarily include the selling of grave sites, book keeping, preparation of Trustee meetings, and burial arrangements when the need arises. You are required to file a Form 700. You are not involved in the price setting for any of the Cemetery plots or services. You also are not involved in any negotiations with cemetery clients.

________________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-16-083
Page No. 2

Pre-need insurance is an insurance covering the opening and closing of the grave, vault, in-
ground vase, marker setting fee, etc. It is for those who want to ensure all costs are paid in
advance. These policies can be paid with one payment but can also be paid with three or five-year
payment arrangements, with all payments being made directly to the insurance company. This
agreement locks in today's cost for the recipient. You would like to know if it would be a conflict
of interest if you offer and ultimately sell Pre-Need insurance to customers who are coming in to
purchase cemetery services. The insurance would be offered through a private company and you
would be paid by that outside company for your sales.

In the 18-months that you have managed the cemetery, you have had families inquire as to
the possibility of purchasing these services in advance.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 of the Act prohibits a public official from making, participating in making, or
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest. A public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental
decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on one
or more of the official's interests. (Section 87103.) The financial interests are:

1. Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect
investment worth two thousand dollars ($2,000) or more. (Section
87103(a).)

2. Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect
interest worth two thousand dollars ($2,000) or more. (Section
87103(b).)

3. Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending
institution made in the regular course of business on terms available to
the public without regard to official status, aggregating five hundred
dollars ($500) or more in value provided or promised to, received by,
the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) This includes commission
income as a broker, agent, or salesperson for a specific sale or similar
transaction. You may have interests in both the direct source of the
income as well as the actual source of the income (for example clients
who purchase the pre-need insurance as well as the insurance
company). (Regulation 18700.1.)

4. Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer,
partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management.
(Section 87103(d).)

5. Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or
gifts aggregating [$460] or more in value provided to, received by, or

File No. A-16-083
Page No. 3

promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made. (Section 87103(e).)

6. Personal finances, meaning the financial effect of a governmental
decision on the personal finances of a public official or his or her
immediate family.


In your case, you would have an economic interest in the pre-need insurance company you
are seeking to work for and any clients who purchase pre-need insurance, assuming that you would
receive income of $500 or more from this company or those clients.

To determine whether you have a conflict of interest under the Act, we must determine
whether decisions you make as Cemetery Manager would have a foreseeable, material financial
effect on the pre-need insurance company. You stated your work as Cemetery Manager includes the
selling of grave sites, book keeping, preparation of Trustee meetings, and burial arrangements when
the need arises. You are not involved in negotiations with client or price setting. The concessions you
would be selling insurance to would be those who are already coming in the cemetery to purchase
services or plots from the cemetery. You have not identified any governmental decisions you make
with regard to these duties that would have a reasonably foreseeable financial effect upon the pre-
need insurance company or your prospective clients. Therefore, you would not have a conflict of
interest under the Political Reform Act. You should however consult with your agency counsel and
the Attorney General about other potential areas of law that could be affected including restrictions
on incompatible activities your agency may impose. Also, should a decision you make in your
capacity as the Cemetery Manager have a potential financial effect upon the pre-need insurance
company or any prospective client please seek further assistance.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By:    Sukhi K. Brar
       Senior Counsel, Legal Division

SKB:jgl

sections.question:
As a Cemetery Manager do the Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit you from 
selling pre-need insurance?
sections.conclusion: No, the Act does not prohibit you from selling pre-need insurance to Cemetery customers.

selling pre-need insurance?

CONCLUSION: No, the Act does not prohibit you from selling pre-need insurance to Cemetery customers.
